title: Inventory quality-surveillance, field-survey, market-distribution, and regulatory data sources for SF medicines analysis
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates WHO GSMS reports, national quality surveillance data, peer-reviewed field surveys, market-distribution records, and regulatory authority datasets into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit assessment of sampling frame, test method, and substandard-versus-falsified disaggregation.
inputs: WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System reports and documentation
WHO 2017 global report on substandard and falsified medical products
Peer-reviewed field-survey studies of medicine quality in LMICs
National medicines regulatory authority inspection and testing records where accessible
Market-distribution data from import records, pharmacy chains, and procurement databases
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each source for URL, date, product category, geographic grain, test method, sampling frame, failure definition, and whether substandard and falsified products are disaggregated.
done_condition: At least six candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering sampling frame, test method, geographic grain, supply-chain tier, and substandard-versus-falsified disaggregation status.

title: Write a reconciliation note separating substandard from falsified failure modes and prohibiting conflated reporting
outcome: A short analytic note that defines substandard and falsified as distinct failure modes, states which surveillance strategy each requires, and which comparisons or aggregate claims are prohibited without disaggregation.
expected_artifact: validation.md and outputs.md updates with failure-mode rules.
validation_method: Reviewer checks that each failure mode is tied to its definition, required test method, surveillance strategy, and known mismatch when aggregated with the other.
done_condition: No comparison is left ambiguous about whether it refers to substandard quality failure, falsified fraudulent mislabeling, or an aggregate that conflates both.

title: Select one to three countries where sub-national medicine-quality surveillance-gap work is genuinely feasible
outcome: A country-selection memo that names which countries have adequate quality-testing data, market-distribution data, regulatory-capacity data, and sub-national identifiers to support a reviewable surveillance-gap analysis.
expected_artifact: validation.md update with inclusion and exclusion rules.
validation_method: Replicator checks that selected countries meet the documented minimum data requirements and that rejected countries have explicit reasons.
done_condition: Every selected country has documented surveillance-laboratory count, testing frequency, product categories tested, market-distribution data availability, sub-national grain, and explicit surveillance-gap assessment.

title: Map post-market surveillance coverage gaps relative to market volume and known risk factors
outcome: A bounded analysis for one selected country that maps where post-market testing density is too low to detect quality failures relative to estimated market volume and product-class risk factors, framed as a coverage-gap assessment rather than a failure-rate prediction.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and validation.md updates with a country-specific surveillance-gap map.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the gap analysis and confirms the testing-density metric, market-volume denominator, product-class risk weighting, and uncertainty statements.
done_condition: The artifact states the exact surveillance-density metric, the market-volume denominator, which districts have no testing, which product classes are untested, what the gap does and does not imply, and why the result is not yet a procurement recommendation.

title: Red-team panic-driven withdrawal, market distortion, and supply-chain gaming
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how surveillance-gap maps could trigger panic-driven market withdrawals that reduce medicine access, enable supply-chain gaming to avoid testing locations, or be weaponized against specific manufacturers without due process.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Mitigations are confirmed or publication is blocked, then a second reviewer replicates the check that each high-risk output is either removed or explicitly qualified with access-harm warnings and gaming-mitigation notes, with replicated red-team review before publication.
done_condition: Every procurement or regulator-facing claim is either blocked, softened to analytic language, or gated behind explicit warnings about panic-driven withdrawal, market distortion, and supply-chain gaming, with replicated red-team review before publication.

title: Check whether outputs match regulator, procurement officer, and supply-chain manager decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the surveillance-gap artifact would change a real decision for a national medicines regulatory authority inspector, a procurement officer, or a supply-chain manager without reducing medicine access or creating gaming incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with named use and non-use cases.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the decision, user, timing, required workflow, and misuse risk, then a second reviewer replicates the check on the stated non-use cases.
done_condition: The artifact has a named user, named decision, named supply-chain tier, explicit non-use cases including access-harm and gaming risks, and replicated field-reality review before publication.
